assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate (convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for comment 30002, no sub-task created for comment 30002); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56 assertions). Repetitive Test Detection: N/A. Test Documentation: N/A. 1 eval failure sub-task created for eval-3. |' — Test Quality is WARN, driven by Eval Quality component being WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 27-31 document eval result detection: 'An eval result review was detected from review ID 40001: Author: github-actions[bot] (matches criterion 1), Marker: Body contains ## Eval Results (matches criterion 2), Footer: Body contains sdlc-workflow/run-evals (matches criterion 3). All three heuristic conditions matched.' Lines 36-44 show the Eval Quality Summary with per-eval pass rates. Line 13 reflects eval metrics in the Test Quality row: 'eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate... overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56 assertions)'.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md classifies comment 50001 as 'code change request' from 'reviewer-b'. Lines 22-23 explicitly state: 'This is a human reviewer comment, not an eval result. The review (id 40002) is from user "reviewer-b" (a human user), not from github-actions[bot], and does not contain the ## Eval Results marker or sdlc-workflow/run-evals footer. It is processed as a normal review comment through the standard classification pipeline.' No eval detection or eval metrics appear in the classification. Report line 54 confirms: '50001 | reviewer-b | code change request | Sub-task created (subtask-1)'.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row contains: 'eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate (convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for comment 30002, no sub-task created for comment 30002); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56 assertions)' — both failing assertion topics (convention upgrade eligibility, sub-task creation) are named in the Test Quality row.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md is titled 'Sub-Task: Fix eval-3 assertion failures: convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation' with Type: eval-failure. It includes two failing assertions with full text and evidence: Failing Assertion 1 about 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002' with evidence 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility'; Failing Assertion 2 about 'Review comment 30002 results in a sub-task regardless of classification path' with evidence 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md lines 29-39 contain '## Review Context' with Eval ID: eval-3, Pass Rate: 85% (11/13 passed, 2 failed). Failing Assertion 1 quotes: 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)...' with Evidence: 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning'. Failing Assertion 2 quotes: 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with Evidence: 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/RHEcosystemAppEng/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 35-36: '## Target PR\nhttps://github.com/RHEcosystemAppEng/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-2.md lines 41-42: '## Target PR\nhttps://github.com/RHEcosystemAppEng/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. Both the review-feedback and eval-failure sub-tasks include the Target PR section with the correct URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | 2 root-cause tasks created: convention gap for Markdown exclusion oversight, implement-task skill gap for convention upgrade documentation |'. The verdict is DONE (not N/A). subtask-4.md is a root-cause task of type 'root-cause' addressing the eval-3 failures specifically: 'Root-cause: implement-task skill should ensure convention upgrade eligibility is always evaluated and documented for suggestion-classified comments'. subtask-3.md addresses the Markdown exclusion convention gap.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report/classification/sub-task description files: report.md, review-50001.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md, subtask-1.md through subtask-4.md. No code files (.ts, .js, .py, .rs, etc.) were created or appear in the outputs. The sub-task files describe changes to be made (Files to Modify sections) but do not make those changes themselves. The report confirms the skill evaluated existing code (Scope Containment PASS, Acceptance Criteria PASS) without modifying it.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: '| Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md |' — compares diff files against task specification, no eval reference. Line 10: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines |' — scans diff for secrets, no eval reference. Line 12: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 7/7 criteria met -- Check 6 scans for new symbols, verifies doc comments per language, produces PASS/WARN/N/A verdicts, output format includes sixth row, verdict mapping updated |' — evaluates criteria against diff content. The 7 criterion files (criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md) each evaluate their criterion against diff content with no reference to eval processing.
